stimulus: “The Wind and the Pine” is a 1902 poem by Edith Kelly. In the poem, a pine tree is portrayed as intending to stand firm and resist the wind: ______
stem: Which quotation from “The Wind and the Pine” most effectively illustrates the claim?

answerOptions.content: “I am the Wind. I sweep the hills; / I carry the seeds where my will tills; / My voice is heard in every glen, / I rush past field and through the fen.”
“I am the Pine. My needles fall / Year by year on the woodland floor; / Their soft brown carpet covers all, / And builds the soil for years to come.”
“I am the Pine, defiant Wind! / I am rooted here against thy storm. / Howl thou loud or whisper low, / I am set here to stand thy blow.”
“I am the Wind. My breath is free; / Even granite must bend to me; / Mountains crumble beneath my song / As ages pass and time goes long.”

rationale:
Choice C is correct because the pine speaks directly to the wind (“defiant Wind!”) and declares that it is “rooted here against thy storm” and “set here to stand thy blow,” clearly conveying its intent to stand firm and resist the wind.

Choice A is incorrect because it is the wind’s speech, not the pine’s. Choice B is incorrect because it focuses on the pine’s needles falling, not on resisting the wind. Choice D is incorrect because it is the wind speaking about its own power.
